description.p: Sarala is a fast, distraction-free WYSIWYG Markdown editor with no preview pane and no split view — the editing surface is the preview. Every paragraph, heading, list, quote, table and code fence is a live block: click into one and it opens to raw Markdown, click away and it renders in place. One parser, one theme, one window — what you see while writing is exactly what exports.
Files are read and written as plain Markdown, so your documents are never locked into a proprietary format.

ul.li: Live block editing — Markdown styles itself as you type, with dimmed syntax markers that render fully on Enter or when the caret moves away
Smart Enter that continues lists, numbering, task items and blockquotes, and auto-closes code fences
GitHub Flavored Markdown: tables, task lists, strikethrough, and syntax-highlighted fenced code
Math with KaTeX (inline and block) and diagrams with Mermaid and D2
Find and replace, an Open Quickly fuzzy finder, and a command palette
Sidebar with a file tree and a live document outline
Export to HTML and PDF, plus docx, odt, rtf, epub, LaTeX and more via Pandoc
Built-in themes: Paper, Graphite, GitHub, Night, Newsprint and Whitey

screenshot.caption: Editing a Markdown document with live blocks, an outline sidebar and the theme palette

description.p: Each Monokai theme variant now has its own distinct signature accent. First release available as a Flatpak on Flathub.
UI polish: Inter font across all platforms, crisp sidebar hover, a stable Recent Files list and icons in the menu bar dropdowns.
Linux font parity with macOS and Windows, and an on-launch check that offers to install a newer release when one is available.
Auto-updater and a range of editor refinements.
Diagram support (Mermaid and D2), export presets and additional themes.
Find and replace, Open Quickly fuzzy finder, and the sidebar file tree and outline.
